title: Integer overflow in DecryptContentCore allocation
problem: When decrypting CMS EnvelopedData, the code adds blockSize to encryptedContent.dataLen without checking for overflow. An attacker could craft a CMS message with encryptedContent.dataLen close to UINT32_MAX, causing integer overflow and allocating a buffer that is too small, leading to heap buffer overflow during decryption.
code:
uint32_t maxPlaintextLen = encInfo->encryptedContent.dataLen + blockSize;
uint8_t *plaintextData = BSL_SAL_Malloc(maxPlaintextLen);

title: Integer overflow in EnvelopedData_DecryptUpdate
problem: The code adds blockSize to input->dataLen and then adds curOutLen without checking for overflow. An attacker could provide large input data sizes to cause integer overflow, leading to undersized buffer allocation and heap overflow.
code:
uint32_t maxOutLen = input->dataLen + blockSize;
uint32_t totalOutLen = curOutLen + maxOutLen;

title: Integer overflow in TransferAccumulatedData
problem: The code adds curOutLen and accDataLen without checking for overflow, which could lead to undersized buffer allocation.

title: Integer overflow in EnvelopedData_EncryptUpdate
problem: The code adds blockSize to input->dataLen and then adds currentLen without checking for overflow. While this is in the encryption path (not parsing), it could still cause issues with very large inputs.
code:
uint32_t newDataLen = input->dataLen + blockSize;
uint32_t totalLen = currentLen + newDataLen;

title: Integer overflow in EncryptContentCore
problem: The code adds blockSize to plaintext->dataLen without checking for overflow. While this is in the encryption path, it could cause issues with very large plaintext inputs.

title: Potential buffer underflow in ParseRecipientList
problem: The code subtracts valueLen from buffLen without verifying that valueLen <= buffLen. If BSL_ASN1_DecodeLen returns a valid valueLen but valueLen > buffLen, this would cause unsigned integer underflow.

title: Integer overflow in FinalizeEncry
problem: The code adds blockSize to currentLen without checking for overflow.

title: Integer overflow in EnvelopedData_DecryptFinal
problem: The code adds blockSize to currentLen without checking for overflow.
